methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $2.0B - $8.6B.

data.narrative:
Labelbox is a Series D–stage AI & Software platform that has raised $189M to date and carries a last post-money valuation of $1.0B+. The relevant comp universe comprises five private peers in identical sector and stage (Wayve, Cohere, Hugging Face, ElevenLabs, Hive AI) plus one public comparable (Palantir), establishing a mixed-maturity reference set for triangulation.

Two methods anchor the valuation analysis. Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement to the $1.0B last post-money baseline, implying $1.5B. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same-stage private companies yields a median valuation of $4.5B, with a range spanning $2.0B (Hive AI) to $8.6B (Wayve). These methods diverge meaningfully, reflecting variance in growth trajectory and capital efficiency across the peer set.

The triangulated range is $1.3B (low), $3.0B (median), and $5.2B (high). The $1.3B–$5.2B spread reflects material uncertainty around whether Labelbox tracks closer to stage-progression norms or to the median peer valuation. Key uncertainties include revenue scale relative to peers, customer concentration, and gross margin sustainability. The current $1.0B+ valuation sits below the triangulated median, suggesting potential upside if growth metrics align with peer cohorts, though confirmation would require revenue and cohort economics visibility.
